description: Build modern Desktop Applications using Windows Presentation Foundation (WPF) and XAML.

slides.title: What is WPF? 🖥️
slides.content:
# Windows Presentation Foundation

A powerful framework for building Windows desktop apps.

## XAML
Uses **eXtensible Application Markup Language** (XML-based) to define the UI, while C# handles the logic.
- **Separation of Concerns:** Designers work on XAML, Developers work on C#.

slides.title: Basic XAML Logic
slides.content:
# Declaring UI 🎨

```xml
<Window x:Class="MyApp.MainWindow"
        Title="My App" Height="300" Width="400">
    <StackPanel>
        <TextBlock Text="Hello WPF!" FontSize="20"/>
        <Button Content="Click Me" Width="100"/>
    </StackPanel>
</Window>
```
`StackPanel` stacks elements vertically.

slides.title: Layout Containers
slides.content:
# Organizing Elements 📐

1.  **Grid:** Most powerful. Uses Rows and Columns.
2.  **StackPanel:** Stacks items horizontally/vertically.
3.  **WrapPanel:** Wraps items to next line if space runs out.
4.  **DockPanel:** Docks items to Top, Bottom, Left, Right.

slides.title: Container Check
slides.content: Which container organizes elements in a tabular structure with rows and cols?

slides.title: Handling Events
slides.content:
# Making it Interactive 🖱️

**XAML:**
```xml
<Button Content="Click Me" Click="Button_Click"/>
```

**C# Code-Behind (.xaml.cs):**
```csharp
private void Button_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e) {
    MessageBox.Show("Button Clicked!");
}
```

slides.title: Data Binding
slides.content:
# The Power of Binding 🔗

Connect UI properties directly to data sources. If data changes, UI updates automatically!

```xml
<TextBox Text="{Binding UserName, UpdateSourceTrigger=PropertyChanged}"/>
<TextBlock Text="{Binding UserName}"/>
```
Typing in the TextBox instantly updates the TextBlock without C# code!

slides.title: MVVM Pattern
slides.content:
# Model-View-ViewModel 🏗️

The standard architecture for WPF.

1.  **Model:** Data (e.g., `User` class).
2.  **View:** UI (XAML).
3.  **ViewModel:** Logic that connects Model to View.

Keeps code clean, testable, and maintainable.

slides.title: Full Example: Counter
Binding Check
slides.content: Which markup extension is used to bind a property to a data source?

slides.title: Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: WPF 🎓

You're now a Desktop Developer!

## Key Takeaways
- **XAML:** Layout language.
- **Code-Behind:** Logic file.
- **Layouts:** Grid, StackPanel.
- **Binding:** Powerful data synchronization.
- **MVVM:** Professional architecture.

This concludes our C# journey. From console apps to full GUI applications, you have the tools to build anything!
